Fee: Free for enrolment/update of demographic details; Rs 50 for biometric update, Rs 50 for PVC card
Processing Time: 90 days for first enrolment (typical); updates 1-90 days
Online Process: Book a slot at an Aadhaar Seva Kendra via appointments.uidai.gov.in. Address-only updates (no biometric change) can be done fully online via myaadhaar.gov.in using OTP + existing Aadhaar.
Offline Process: Visit nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra / bank-run Aadhaar centre / post office Aadhaar counter. Fill enrolment/update form, biometric capture (photo, iris, 10 fingerprints) for new enrolment, submit ID+address proof, collect acknowledgement slip with URN.
Required Documents: New enrolment: any 1 valid PoI (Passport/PAN/Voter ID/DL) + 1 valid PoA (utility bill/bank statement/ration card) OR birth certificate for minors OR proof from head of family.

Issuing Office: Income Tax Dept (via NSDL/Protean or UTIITSL)
Processing Time: 15-20 days (physical); ~48 hrs for e-PAN via Aadhaar (instant e-PAN is free)
Online Process: Apply via onlineservices.nsdl.com (Protean/NSDL) or utiitsl.com. Fill Form 49A (Indian citizen), upload photo/signature, pay fee online, e-sign/e-KYC via Aadhaar OTP.
Offline Process: Download Form 49A, fill manually, attach photo+ID+address proof, submit at nearest PAN Facilitation/TIN-FC centre with fee in cash/DD.
Required Documents: Proof of Identity (Aadhaar/Passport/Voter ID), Proof of Address (Aadhaar/utility bill/bank statement), Proof of Date of Birth (birth certificate/Matriculation certificate), passport-size photo.

Issuing Office: District Election Officer / Electoral Registration Officer (via State CEO)
Department: Election Commission of India (via State CEO)
Online Process: Register/apply via voters.eci.gov.in or Voter Helpline app using Form 6 (new elector) or Form 8 (correction). Upload photo and documents, submit online; a Booth Level Officer (BLO) verifies via field visit.
Offline Process: Submit filled Form 6/8 physically at the Electoral Registration Officer (ERO)/Assistant ERO office or to the local BLO.
Required Documents: Proof of age (birth certificate/10th marksheet/Aadhaar), proof of ordinary residence (Aadhaar/utility bill/rent agreement/bank passbook), recent passport-size photo.

Issuing Office: Regional Passport Office / Passport Seva Kendra
Processing Time: 30-45 days normal; 1-3 days Tatkaal (after police clearance)
Online Process: Register on portal2.passportindia.gov.in, fill application (fresh/reissue), pay fee online, book appointment at nearest Passport Seva Kendra (PSK)/Passport Seva Laghu Kendra (PSLK), visit for document verification, photo & biometrics.
Offline Process: Applicants who cannot self-file online can use Common Service Centres (CSC) to fill and submit the application on their behalf; a final in-person appointment/visit to PSK is mandatory either way.
Required Documents: Proof of Address (Aadhaar/utility bill/rent agreement), Proof of Date of Birth (birth certificate/PAN/10th certificate), Aadhaar (for e-KYC), old passport (for reissue).

Fee: LL Rs 150-200; Permanent DL Rs 200-500 depending on vehicle class
Processing Time: LL: instant-7 days; Permanent DL: 30 days after LL, min 30-day gap
Online Process: Apply via sarathi.parivahan.gov.in - fill Learner's Licence (LL) form, take the LL test online from home after Aadhaar authentication; for the permanent DL, book a slot for the driving test at the RTO after the LL is 30+ days old.
Offline Process: Visit RTO in person, fill Form 4 (LL)/Form 4 (DL after passing test), submit documents, undergo the driving test physically at the RTO test track.
Required Documents: Proof of age & address (Aadhaar/Passport/Voter ID), passport photo, self-declaration of fitness (Form 1)/medical certificate (Form 1A) for transport licences.

Fee: Rs 300 (two-wheeler) / Rs 600 (car); duplicate RC = 50% of original fee; road tax separate (9-13% of ex-showroom for cars)
Online Process: New vehicle: dealer files Form 20 electronically via Vahan (vahan.parivahan.gov.in) on the buyer's behalf using e-KYC. For existing-owner services (address change, duplicate RC, hypothecation), apply via Vahan citizen services with Aadhaar OTP e-sign.
Offline Process: Visit the jurisdictional RTO, submit Form 20 (new registration)/Form 25 (renewal)/Form 29+30 (transfer)/Form 26 or 28 (duplicate) with documents and fee at the counter; vehicle physically inspected by a Motor Vehicle Inspector for new registration.
Required Documents: Sale invoice/Form 21, PUC certificate, insurance certificate, valid ID & address proof, Form 22/22A, temporary registration (if any), Aadhaar for e-KYC.

Department: Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Protection Dept
Fee: Free (nominal CSC/seva-kendra service charge may apply)
Online Process: Apply via Maharashtra's Aaple Sarkar portal (aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in) under Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Protection Dept - new ration card / member addition / correction; upload documents, pay nominal fee online, track status.
Offline Process: Submit application at the Taluka Supply Office / Ration Office along with documents; verification done by the local Ration Inspector before card issue.
Required Documents: Aadhaar of all family members, address proof, income certificate (for BPL/AAY categories), passport photos, surrender certificate, bank passbook.

Online Process: No dedicated standalone online BPL-card application exists in Maharashtra; BPL status is derived from the state's periodic BPL/SECC survey and reflected in the Ration Card/AAY category via Aaple Sarkar.
Offline Process: Apply for inclusion/correction in the BPL list at the Gram Panchayat (rural)/Ward office (urban) or Taluka Supply Office, supported by an income and asset declaration; local verification required.
Required Documents: Income certificate, family details/ration card, caste certificate (if relevant), self-declaration of assets and occupation.

Online Process: Apply via aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in under Revenue Department -> 'Caste Certificate'. Fill form, upload documents, pay fee online; application routed to the Sub-Divisional Officer/Deputy Collector for verification & digital signature.
Offline Process: Submit application with documents at the Setu Kendra / Tehsildar office of the taluka; verification by Talathi/Circle Officer before the Deputy Collector issues the certificate.
Required Documents: School leaving certificate showing caste, old caste certificate of father/blood relative (if available), domicile/residence proof, affidavit for cases without documentary proof, Aadhaar.

Online Process: Apply via aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in -> Revenue Dept -> 'Domicile Certificate (Residence Certificate)'; upload proof of 15-year continuous residence, submit online, tracked to the Tehsildar for approval.
Offline Process: Submit paper application with residence proof at the Setu Kendra/Tehsildar office of the taluka of residence.
Required Documents: Proof of residence for 15 years (ration card history/school certificates/property documents), Aadhaar, affidavit of residence, birth certificate.

Online Process: Apply via aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in -> Revenue Dept -> 'Income Certificate'; fill income details, upload salary slips/Form 16 or self-employment declaration, submit online for Tehsildar verification.
Offline Process: Submit application with income proof at the Setu Kendra/Tehsildar office.
Required Documents: Salary certificate/Form 16 (salaried) or self-declared income affidavit + supporting proof, Aadhaar, ration card.

Online Process: Apply via aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in -> Revenue Dept -> 'EWS Certificate' (for the 10% EWS reservation, valid one financial year); submit income & asset declaration online.
Offline Process: Submit application with income/asset proof at the Setu Kendra/Tehsildar office; verified by the Circle Officer/Tehsildar.
Required Documents: Income certificate/affidavit (family income below Rs 8 lakh/year), land holding declaration (below 5 acres), residential proof.

Issuing Office: District Medical Board (assessment) + DEPwD (central UDID portal)
Fee: Free (base process); nominal charge up to Rs 50 in some cases
Online Process: Apply via the central Unique Disability ID portal (swavlambancard.gov.in / UDID portal) - register, fill disability details, upload documents, book an appointment at a Government Medical Board for assessment; certificate & UDID card generated online after approval.
Offline Process: Apply at the District Civil Hospital / designated Medical Board centre with the disability certificate application form and supporting medical records; a physical assessment by the Medical Board is mandatory.
Required Documents: Aadhaar, passport photo, existing medical records/prior disability certificate (if any), address proof.

Online Process: Apply via aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in -> Social Justice Dept -> 'Senior Citizen Certificate/Card' (age 60+); upload age proof, submit online.
Offline Process: Submit application with age proof at the Tehsildar office/Setu Kendra, or via the Municipal Corporation's senior-citizen cell in urban areas.
Required Documents: Age proof (Aadhaar/birth certificate/school leaving certificate), address proof, passport photo.

Fee: Stamp duty 3-7% of property value (Gram Panchayat ~3%, Municipal Council ~4%, Municipal Corp ~5-6%; women get 1% concession) + Registration fee 1% capped at Rs 30,000
Processing Time: Same-day at Sub-Registrar office; must register within 4 months of execution
Online Process: Draft the deed (via lawyer/registered document writer), pay stamp duty online via GRAS (gras.mahakosh.gov.in), book a slot via the IGR Maharashtra portal (igrmaharashtra.gov.in); both parties + witnesses must then appear in person at the Sub-Registrar office.
Offline Process: Visit the Sub-Registrar office of the jurisdiction where the property is located with the deed, pay stamp duty & registration fee at the counter, complete biometric verification of buyer/seller/witnesses; document scanned & registered same day.
Required Documents: Original sale deed draft, PAN of both parties, Aadhaar of both parties + 2 witnesses, property card/7-12 extract, previous title deed, society NOC (if applicable), proof of stamp duty payment.

Online Process: Apply via IGR Maharashtra's e-Search (freesearchigrservice.maharashtra.gov.in) for an informal search, or the formal EC application via igrmaharashtra.gov.in - enter property/survey details and period, pay fee online, download the EC digitally signed by the Sub-Registrar.
Offline Process: Apply at the Sub-Registrar office of the jurisdiction with property details and the requisite fee; EC issued after a manual search of registers for older records not yet digitised.
Required Documents: Property/survey number, village/taluka/district details, period for which the EC is sought, applicant's ID proof.

Processing Time: Same day online / few days offline
Online Process: Download free via Mahabhulekh (bhulekh.mahabhumi.gov.in) - select district/taluka/village and survey number to view/download the 7/12 extract; an official Rs 15 digitally-signed copy is available via the Aaple Sarkar/Mahabhumi DSC service.
Offline Process: Obtain a certified physical copy from the Talathi (village accountant) of the concerned Saza/circle office.
Required Documents: Survey/Gat number, village name, applicant ID proof (for a certified copy request via Aaple Sarkar).

Online Process: File the mutation request (Ferfar) via aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in -> Revenue Dept services after a registered sale deed/inheritance event; under the e-Ferfar integration, Sub-Registrar registration data is meant to auto-flow to the Talathi.
Offline Process: Submit the mutation application (with the registered deed copy) to the Talathi of the village; the Talathi issues a notice (Form VI) to interested parties, and after the mandatory objection period, the mutation entry (Ferfar) is approved and the 7/12 updated.
Required Documents: Registered sale deed/inheritance documents (death certificate + legal heir certificate for inheritance), current 7/12 extract, property tax receipt, applicant ID proof.

Issuing Office: Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC/MCGM) - Mumbai; respective ULB elsewhere
Fee: Based on Capital Value System (post-2016 construction) or Rateable Value (pre-2016); properties under 500 sq.ft carpet area fully exempt in Mumbai; 500-700 sq.ft get 60% concession
Processing Time: Instant online receipt on payment; annual due date 30 June (Mumbai)
Online Process: Pay online via the relevant Municipal Corporation's own portal - e.g. Mumbai's portal.mcgm.gov.in (Property Tax); other cities use their own Municipal Corporation e-payment portal, or Aaple Sarkar for smaller Nagar Parishads.
Offline Process: Pay at the Municipal Corporation/Council ward office property tax counter or an authorised bank branch/kiosk; receipt issued on the spot.
Required Documents: Property/PAN (Property Account Number) or old receipt for reference, owner ID proof (for name change/updates).

Fee: Not confirmed (commonly: stamp duty ~0.25% of avg annual rent + deposit)
Online Process: Register the rent/leave-and-licence agreement online via IGR Maharashtra's e-registration (efilingigr.maharashtra.gov.in) - draft the agreement, pay stamp duty via GRAS; biometric of parties may be captured by a visiting official.
Offline Process: Visit the Sub-Registrar office with the drafted rent/leave-and-licence agreement, pay stamp duty & registration fee, complete biometric verification in person.
Required Documents: Draft agreement, Aadhaar/PAN of landlord & tenant, property documents (7/12 or Index-II/property tax receipt), passport photos, 2 witnesses.

Fee: Rs 10/sq.m of land (min Rs 10,000, max Rs 10,00,000); plotted development Rs 5/sq.m; Agent: Rs 10,000 (individual) / Rs 1,00,000 (company)
Processing Time: Typically within 30 days of complete application
Online Process: Apply via maharera.mahaonline.gov.in - the promoter registers the project, uploads title documents/sanctioned plans/architect-engineer-CA certificates, pays the registration fee online, and the certificate is issued digitally on approval.
Offline Process: MahaRERA registration is designed to be filed fully online; promoters without internet access typically use a professional consultant or CSC to file the same online-portal application on their behalf.
Required Documents: Title report/legal title documents, sanctioned building plans & layout, commencement certificate, architect/engineer/CA certificates (Form 1/2/3), project details, promoter PAN/company documents, proforma agreement for sale.

Fee: Set by individual society bylaws, not government
Online Process: No dedicated state government online portal exists for Society NOC - it is issued directly by the housing society's managing committee, typically requested via a written application.
Offline Process: Submit a written application to the Society Secretary/Managing Committee requesting the NOC (for sale, rent, loan, or renovation), clear any outstanding dues, and obtain the NOC on society letterhead with committee signature and seal.
Required Documents: No-dues certificate from the society, share certificate, proof of ownership, ID proof, purpose letter (sale/rent/loan/renovation).

Issuing Office: Jurisdictional GST Officer (State or Central, system-allocated)
Processing Time: 3-7 working days typical; up to 30 days with physical verification
Online Process: Apply via the central GST portal (services.gst.gov.in) - fill Part A/B of REG-01, upload PAN, address proof, bank details, photo; Aadhaar authentication or physical verification by a GST officer; ARN generated, GSTIN issued after approval.
Offline Process: GST registration is designed as an online-only process; applicants unable to self-file typically use a GST Suvidha Provider/Chartered Accountant/CSC to complete the same online form on their behalf.
Required Documents: PAN of business/proprietor, Aadhaar, proof of business registration/constitution, address proof of place of business, bank account statement/cancelled cheque, photograph, digital signature (for companies/LLPs).

Issuing Office: Labour Department / Municipal Corporation (varies by ULB)
Processing Time: Auto-approved if not rejected within 60 days (Ease of Doing Business rule)
Online Process: Apply via aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in / the Labour Dept's Shop & Establishment online registration - fill establishment details, upload documents, pay a fee based on employee-count slab, certificate issued digitally.
Offline Process: Submit application at the local Municipal Corporation/Labour office (Facilitator) with documents; verification and issue handled offline for areas without full portal access.
Required Documents: Proof of establishment address, PAN, employer ID proof, employee details/count, constitution proof (partnership deed/company incorporation, if applicable).

Fee: Varies by ULB and trade category
Portal: Varies by ULB (e.g. mcgm.gov.in for Mumbai)
Online Process: Apply via the relevant Municipal Corporation's e-portal or Aaple Sarkar - e.g. Mumbai's MCGM portal for trade licences under specific categories.
Offline Process: Submit application with documents at the Municipal Corporation/Council's Licence Department counter; site inspection by a Health/Licence Inspector before issue.
Required Documents: Property/shop documents, ID proof of applicant, NOC from Fire Dept (for applicable trades), Shop & Establishment registration, layout plan.

Online Process: Apply via the central Udyam Registration portal (udyamregistration.gov.in) using Aadhaar (of proprietor/authorised signatory) and PAN; self-declare investment & turnover, certificate generated instantly online, no fee.
Offline Process: Udyam Registration is a fully online, self-declaration process by design; applicants without internet access can use a CSC to complete the same online form.
Required Documents: Aadhaar of proprietor/partner/director, PAN of business/individual, bank account details, basic business activity (NIC code) information.

Issuing Office: FSSAI (Basic Registration/State Licence) via FoSCoS/FLRS portal; Central Licence direct with FSSAI
Department: FSSAI (Central) - State FSSAI office for Basic/State tier
Fee: Basic: Rs 100/yr; State Licence: Rs 2,000-5,000/yr; Central Licence: Rs 7,500/yr
Online Process: Apply via the central FSSAI portal (foodlicensing.fssai.gov.in) - choose Basic Registration/State Licence/Central Licence based on scale, fill Form A/B, upload documents, pay fee online. April 2026 rule change: renewed FSSAI licences valid perpetually rather than requiring periodic renewal.
Offline Process: Applicants without internet access can approach a CSC or an FSSAI-empanelled consultant to file the same online application.
Required Documents: Photo ID of proprietor/partners, proof of business premises, food safety management plan (for State/Central licence), water testing report (in some cases), NOC from the municipality.

Department: Maharashtra Goods and Services Tax Department
Fee: Registration free; PTEC liability max Rs 2,500/year; PTRC per employee salary slab
Processing Time: Register within 30 days of starting business/hiring
Online Process: Apply via the Maharashtra GST Department's Mahagst portal (mahagst.gov.in) - PTEC (for the business/professional entity) and PTRC (for deducting employee PT) registration filed online with PAN and business details; certificate issued digitally.
Offline Process: Registration is intended to be filed online; entities without internet access typically use a tax consultant/CA to file the same online application on their behalf.
Required Documents: PAN of business/professional, proof of business address, bank account details, employee salary details (for PTRC), incorporation/partnership documents.

Issuing Office: Chief Fire Officer (via Divisional Fire Officer)
Fee: No department fee at base level per some sources; Fire Protection Fund fee applies for MIDC-area/larger proposals (variable)
Online Process: Apply via mahafireservice.gov.in's e-Fire Approval/Single Window portal - new registration, upload architect-approved plans and fire-safety system layout, pay the Fire Protection Fund fee, provisional NOC issued online, final NOC issued after physical site inspection.
Offline Process: Apply at the Divisional Fire Officer's office of the local Municipal Corporation/MIDC (for industrial estates) with physical copies of plans and documents; a site inspection is mandatory in person.
Required Documents: Approved building plan, fire-safety system layout & equipment list, electrical wiring compliance certificate, occupancy certificate (for existing buildings), Form A/B declarations under the Maharashtra Fire Prevention and Life Safety Measures Act 2006.

Issuing Office: Commissioner of State Excise / District Superintendent of State Excise
Fee: Highly category-dependent: permit room ~Rs 5,44,000/yr, beer shop ~Rs 1,50,000/yr, restaurant/bar commonly cited Rs 10-25 lakh range in Mumbai
Processing Time: 21-day public notice period + inspection; 30-60 days overall
Online Process: Apply via the Maharashtra State Excise Department's online portal (excise.maharashtra.gov.in) for the relevant licence category (FL-II/FL-III retail, CL-III bar, wine shop, etc.); upload documents, pay a category-specific fee, verification by the district Excise Superintendent.
Offline Process: Submit application at the District Excise Superintendent's office with documents; site inspection and police verification conducted before licence grant.
Required Documents: Proof of premises ownership/lease, character/police verification certificate, ID proof, NOC from the local body, distance-from-institution compliance certificate, financial solvency proof.

Department: Reserve Bank of India (Central, via Central KYC Registry)
Online Process: Most banks now allow online KYC via video-KYC (V-CIP) or Aadhaar-based e-KYC through the bank's mobile app/net-banking at account opening or periodic KYC update.
Offline Process: Submit a physical KYC form with self-attested ID and address proof copies at the bank branch; in-person original verification (IPV) done by the branch official.
Required Documents: PAN or Form 60, Aadhaar/Passport/Voter ID/DL (as an officially valid document for identity+address), recent photograph.

Online Process: File via the Income Tax Department's e-filing portal (eportal.incometax.gov.in) - select the applicable ITR form, pre-filled data auto-populates from AIS/26AS, verify and submit, e-verify via Aadhaar OTP/net-banking/DSC.
Offline Process: Taxpayers without internet access can file through a Tax Return Preparer (TRP) or CA who submits the same return via the online portal.
Required Documents: PAN, Aadhaar, Form 16 (salaried)/Form 26AS/AIS, bank account details for refund, investment proofs for deductions, business/professional income records if applicable.

Fee: Free to file; late fee Rs 50/day (Rs 20/day for nil returns)
Online Process: File via the GST portal (services.gst.gov.in) - GSTR-1 (outward supplies) and GSTR-3B (summary return with tax payment) filed monthly/quarterly using GSTIN login, auto-populated data from e-invoices where applicable, submitted with DSC/EVC.
Offline Process: GST returns are an online-only filing system; businesses without internet access typically use a GST practitioner/CA.
Required Documents: GSTIN login credentials, sales/purchase invoice data, e-way bill data (if applicable), input tax credit records, DSC or EVC via OTP.

Document Name: Police Verification for Hotel Guests (incl. C-Form)
Issuing Office: Local Police Station (jurisdiction of the hotel)
Fee: No separate government fee for the C-Form filing itself
Processing Time: C-Form must be submitted within 24 hours of a foreign guest's arrival
Online Process: Hotels/lodges register foreign-national guest stays via the Foreigners Regional Registration Office's C-Form online module (indianfrro.gov.in) within 24 hours of check-in.
Offline Process: For Indian guests, police verification is typically done via the local police station's manual guest-register submission by the hotel; smaller establishments still submit physical guest registers periodically to the local police station.
Required Documents: Guest passport & visa details (foreign nationals) or ID proof (domestic guests), hotel/lodge registration certificate, guest arrival/departure register.

Department: Maharashtra Police Dept + Urban Local Body
Portal: Varies by city (e.g. Mumbai Police licensing portal)
Online Process: Apply via the relevant Municipal Corporation's licence portal (e.g. MCGM's Eating House Licence process) along with the Police (Eating House) Licence from the local Police Commissionerate's portal where digitised.
Offline Process: Submit application at the Municipal Corporation's Licence Department and separately at the local Police Station/Commissionerate's Licensing branch; site inspection required.
Required Documents: Shop & Establishment registration, FSSAI licence, Fire NOC, property documents, ID proof, police character verification of proprietor/staff.

Processing Time: Instant on employer registration via UAN portal
Online Process: UAN generation/activation via the EPFO Member portal (unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in) using the Aadhaar-linked UAN sent by the employer; activate via OTP, link Aadhaar/bank/PAN via e-KYC for online claims.
Offline Process: Employer submits the employee's UAN/PF registration via the employer's EPFO portal login; this is inherently an employer-driven online process.
Required Documents: Aadhaar, PAN, bank account & IFSC, employer's PF code, Form 11 (declaration by new employee) submitted to the employer.

Department: Employees' State Insurance Corporation (Central; dispensary network is state/region-wise)
Fee: Employee contributes 0.75% of wages, employer 3.25% (no separate card fee)
Online Process: ESIC registration is employer-driven via the ESIC portal (esic.gov.in) at the time of employee onboarding using Aadhaar; the e-Pehchan card is generated and downloadable online by the employee.
Offline Process: No separate offline registration route for employees; the employer registers the employee via the ESIC online portal as part of statutory compliance.
Required Documents: Aadhaar, bank account details, employer's ESIC establishment code, employee photograph, family details (for dependents' coverage).

Issuing Office: Labour Commissioner's Office (registering authority for the Board)
Department: Maharashtra Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
Online Process: Apply via the Maharashtra Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board's online portal (mahabocw.in) - register as a construction worker, upload proof of 90 days' work in construction in the preceding year, pay a nominal registration fee online.
Offline Process: Submit application with supporting documents at the district Labour Office/BOCW Welfare Board office; site/employer verification of the 90-day work claim.
Required Documents: Age proof, proof of 90 days construction work (employer certificate/site details), Aadhaar, bank account details, passport photo.

Issuing Office: State Health Assurance Society / Aarogyamitra (at empanelled hospitals)
Department: Maharashtra Public Health Dept (state scheme integrated with central AB-PMJAY)
Processing Time: Same day at hospital counter via Aarogyamitra facilitator
Online Process: Check eligibility/apply via the National Health Authority's Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY portal (beneficiary.nha.gov.in) using Aadhaar/ration card; in Maharashtra the scheme runs as the integrated AB-MJPJAY via jeevandayee.gov.in - check eligibility and download the e-card.
Offline Process: Visit the nearest empanelled hospital's Arogya Mitra help-desk or a Common Service Centre with the ration card/Aadhaar for eligibility verification and physical card issuance.
Required Documents: Aadhaar, ration card (the primary family-identification document for AB-MJPJAY in Maharashtra), income proof (for certain categories).

Department: IRDAI (Central regulator; not a government-issued document)
Fee: N/A - cost is the insurance premium, not a document fee
Processing Time: Issued on policy purchase (instant to few days)
Portal: Individual insurer portals; irdai.gov.in for regulation
Online Process: Most private/public health insurers issue e-cards/policy documents digitally via their own portal or app immediately after policy issuance.
Offline Process: A physical card/policy document is couriered by the insurer after an offline policy purchase through an agent/branch.
Required Documents: Proposal form, ID & address proof, age proof, medical history declaration, premium payment proof.

Issuing Office: Notary Public (appointed under the Notaries Act by State or Central Govt)
Department: Department of Justice / State Law Dept
Fee: Stamp paper value + notary fee (commonly Rs 100-500 stamp paper + notary charge)
Portal: N/A - done in person with a licensed notary
Online Process: Some affidavit categories can now use e-Stamping (SHCIL's e-stamp portal, or GRAS for Maharashtra) for the stamp-paper component, but the actual notarisation still requires an in-person visit to a licensed Notary.
Offline Process: Draft the affidavit, purchase stamp paper (physical or e-stamp), sign in the presence of a licensed Notary Public who verifies identity and affixes seal/signature.
Required Documents: ID proof (Aadhaar/PAN), the drafted affidavit text, appropriate stamp paper value, notary's fee.
